green/welcome/juniper: Hey there 👋 Let’s set today up with clarity.
green/s1-mind-scan/juniper: What are 1–3 things at the top of your mind today?
green/s1-covey-sort/juniper: Sort these into Covey’s Matrix: Important/Urgent, Not Urgent/Important, etc.
green/s1-expand/juniper: What other small or big to-dos are floating in your head?
green/s1-covey-sort-2/juniper: Sort these into Covey buckets again.
green/s2-align/juniper: Which one best ties into your Weekly Horizon?
green/s2-align/tip.title: What makes something *important but not urgent*?
green/s3-time/juniper:
Let’s look at your calendar to see how much time you can commit to it.

How much open time do you have?
green/s3-time/tip.title: What is a Pomodoro? What is time-blocking?
green/s4-align-day/juniper: Let’s make sure your day actually supports this plan.
green/s4-align-day/tip.body: Real-life alignment reduces overwhelm and builds flow.
green/s5-checkin/juniper: Where’s your head at now that we’ve made this plan?
green/s5-checkin/option:nervous: 😬 Ready to do this... but just a little nervous
green/s5-checkin/option:overwhelmed: 🌀 Optimistic but now feeling kinda overwhelmed
green/s5-checkin/option:processing: 🔄 I’m processing all the changes we made to my original plan for the day
green/s5-processing/juniper: Want to walk through WHY we made these changes?
green/s5-processing/option:no-continue: No, I’m good — let’s keep going
green/s5-processing-help/juniper: Does that help? Or is there something I’m still missing that’s important to you?
green/s5-processing-missing/juniper: Tell me what’s important to you that I’m not seeing yet.
green/s6-goal/juniper: Which goal will you be working on today?
green/s6-calendar/juniper: Want it added to your calendar — plus future steps if we break it down?
green/s6-reflect/juniper.evening: Let’s close the loop on your day.
green/s6-evening-tomorrow/juniper: What’s one small thing you can do tonight to make tomorrow easier?
green/s6-habit-stack/juniper: Have you already stacked a habit around this? Or could you try—like reading 15 minutes after you plug in your phone?
green/fallback-flip/juniper: What do you *not* want to feel today?
green/fallback-still-stuck/juniper: Want to look back at your Weekly Horizon, or take a quick walk or stretch and come back?
green/mini-reset/juniper: Need a breather before we move forward?
yellow/s1/juniper: Sometimes our brain hits the brakes even when we know the next step. Let’s figure out what’s slowing you down.
yellow/s1/response: You’re not broken. Feeling stuck doesn’t mean you’re lazy—it just means something’s in the way. Let’s explore it.
yellow/s1/option:physical: 😩 I’m tired, hungry, or just physically off.
yellow/s1/option:emotional: 😤 I’m carrying something emotionally—frustrated, anxious, or just heavy.
yellow/s1/option:overload: 😵 There’s too much going on—I don’t know where to start.
yellow/s1/option:procrastination: ⏳ I’ve been procrastinating and I don’t even know why.
yellow/s1/option:frozen: 🧊 I don’t even know. I just feel frozen.
yellow/s2a/juniper:
Your body’s talking to you—and you listened. That’s a win.

Want to take a moment for a quick reset before we keep going?
yellow/s2a/response: Let’s honor your effort to check in. When you're ready, we’ll make a plan you can actually stick to.
yellow/s2b/juniper:
Heavy feelings make it hard to move—but naming them is already a step forward.

You don’t have to fix how you feel to take action. Want to talk it out, or just keep going with that in mind?
yellow/s2b/option:carry: Keep going with that in mind
yellow/s2b-pause/juniper: ✏️ Write down one word for how you feel
yellow/s2b-carry/juniper: Let’s carry it with care. You’re still allowed to move toward what matters.
yellow/s2c/juniper:
Too much to do makes everything harder to start. Let’s dump it out and sort it.

What’s taking up space in your brain today? Let it all out—big or small.
yellow/s2c-sort/juniper: Let’s sort these into Covey’s 4 quadrants: Urgent/Important, Not Urgent/Important, etc.
yellow/s2c-align/juniper: Which of these feel most aligned with what matters to you today?
yellow/s2d/juniper:
Procrastination is usually protecting you from something—it’s not a flaw.

Let’s figure out what kind of resistance this is.
yellow/s2d-reframe/juniper: You don’t need to win—you just need to begin.
yellow/s2e/juniper:
Frozen doesn’t mean broken. It just means overwhelmed.

Let’s try one tiny action—just enough to create movement.
yellow/s2e/response: You didn’t avoid the stuck—you showed up for it. That counts.
yellow/s2e/option:one-word: ✏️ Write down one word for how you feel
yellow/s2e/option:tidy: 🕯️ Tidy one corner of your space
yellow/s3/juniper:
Let’s make this doable—not ideal. We’re working with real time and real energy today.

How much time and energy do you realistically have today?
yellow/s3/response: Whatever option you choose—we’ll work with it. Tiny steps count.
yellow/s3/option:not-sure: 🤔 Not sure—help me figure that out
yellow/s3-unsure/juniper: Take a quick glance at your calendar—and at your energy. Which of these feels real for today?
yellow/s4/juniper:
Let’s take a sec to check what your day actually looks like—either in your calendar or in your head.

What’s already planned today that we should work around?
yellow/s4-options/juniper: Making your plan match your actual day is a power move—not a compromise.
yellow/s4-options/option:buffer: ⛅ Add buffer time before/after focus task
yellow/s4-options/option:remove: 🧽 Remove a non-essential task if needed
yellow/s4-options/option:ritual: 🔁 Pair the new task with a ritual (music, stretch, tea)
yellow/s4-options/option:block: 📅 Block the time visually to lock it in
yellow/s5/juniper: Before we dive in—how are you feeling heading into this?
yellow/s5-first-step/juniper: What’s the very first step you can take? Something light, visible, and real.
yellow/s5-first-step/tip.body: First steps should take under 2 minutes. Just enough to shift the momentum.
yellow/s6/juniper:
You made it to the other side of stuck. That deserves reflection.

Which of these feels true right now?
yellow/s6/option:adjusted: 🔄 I adjusted but still showed up
yellow/s6/option:clearer: 🧠 I didn’t finish, but I feel clearer
yellow/s6/option:still-here: ⚠️ I couldn’t really start, but I’m still here
yellow/s6/option:aha: ✨ I had a breakthrough or Aha! moment
yellow/s6-quote/juniper: Which of these feels true to carry with you?
yellow/s6-aha-offer/juniper: Want to save an Aha! moment from today—something that clicked?
yellow/s6-aha-text/juniper: What clicked? A sentence is plenty.
yellow/fallback-flip/juniper: What do you *not* want to feel today?
yellow/fallback-still-stuck/juniper: Want to look back at your Weekly Horizon, or take a quick walk or stretch and come back?
quotes/green/standard/0: I didn’t shut down—I actually faced it.
quotes/green/standard/1: I made space for how I feel and made a plan.
quotes/green/standard/2: I can start over anytime I need to.
quotes/green/standard/3: I showed up anyway—chaos and all.
quotes/green/standard/4: I kinda crushed that for someone who was spiraling 30 minutes ago.
quotes/green/standard/5: I made decisions Future Me will thank me for.
quotes/green/evening/0: I didn’t shut down—I actually faced it.
quotes/green/evening/1: I made space for how I feel and made a plan.
quotes/green/evening/2: I can start over anytime I need to.
quotes/green/evening/4: I showed up anyway—chaos and all.
quotes/green/evening/5: I made decisions Future Me will thank me for.
quotes/yellow/standard/0: I made space for what felt messy—and that helped me move.
quotes/yellow/standard/1: The fog didn’t lift all at once, but I kept walking.
quotes/yellow/standard/2: I asked for help instead of shutting down.
quotes/yellow/standard/3: I stayed in the process—even when I didn’t feel ready.
quotes/yellow/standard/4: Naming what was hard helped me find what mattered.
quotes/yellow/standard/5: Today I planned with honesty—not pressure.
quotes/yellow/standard/6: I didn’t skip the hard part—I walked through it.
quotes/yellow/standard/7: I moved something forward without being ‘on point.’ That’s progress.
quotes/yellow/standard/8: I followed the thread—and clarity found me.
quotes/yellow/standard/9: Even when I didn’t feel strong, I stayed open to movement.
quotes/yellow/standard/10: I let today be a reset—not a redo.
quotes/yellow/standard/11: I asked myself what I needed—and that changed everything.
quotes/yellow/standard/12: I trusted the system even when I wasn’t sure I trusted myself.
quotes/yellow/standard/13: The plan isn’t perfect—but it’s mine.
quotes/yellow/standard/14: I didn’t try to force clarity—I gave it space to emerge.
quotes/yellow/evening/0: I made space for what felt messy—and that helped me move.
quotes/yellow/evening/1: The fog didn’t lift all at once, but I kept walking.
quotes/yellow/evening/2: I asked for help instead of shutting down.
quotes/yellow/evening/3: I stayed in the process—even when I didn’t feel ready.
quotes/yellow/evening/4: Naming what was hard helped me find what mattered.
quotes/yellow/evening/5: Today I planned with honesty—not pressure.
quotes/yellow/evening/6: I didn’t skip the hard part—I walked through it.
quotes/yellow/evening/7: I moved something forward without being ‘on point.’ That’s progress.
quotes/yellow/evening/8: I followed the thread—and clarity found me.
quotes/yellow/evening/9: Even when I didn’t feel strong, I stayed open to movement.
quotes/yellow/evening/10: I let today be a reset—not a redo.
quotes/yellow/evening/11: I asked myself what I needed—and that changed everything.
quotes/yellow/evening/12: I trusted the system even when I wasn’t sure I trusted myself.
quotes/yellow/evening/13: The plan isn’t perfect—but it’s mine.
quotes/yellow/evening/14: I didn’t try to force clarity—I gave it space to emerge.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/0: Step outside for 3 minutes and take a deep breath.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/1: Do 5 jumping jacks or a light stretch.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/2: Drink a full glass of water.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/3: Write down 3 things on your mind and set them aside.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/4: Text someone you trust just to say hi.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/5: Look at a calming image or video for 60 seconds.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/6: Pet your dog/cat or hug something soft.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/7: Listen to one favorite song start to finish.
mini-reset/green/section/0/action/8: Do one small thing you can check off (e.g. throw trash away, plug in phone).
mini-reset/green/reentry: Feeling a little more grounded? Let’s head back in and choose something small to begin with.
mini-reset/yellow/section/0/action/0: Take three deep breaths, stretching your arms up as you inhale.
mini-reset/yellow/section/0/action/1: Run cold or warm water over your hands and focus on the feeling.
mini-reset/yellow/section/0/action/2: Light a candle, turn on a lamp, or adjust your lighting to refresh your space.
mini-reset/yellow/section/0/action/3: Stand up and gently stretch while looking out a window.
mini-reset/yellow/section/0/action/4: Sip a drink slowly—notice the temperature, the flavor, the moment.
mini-reset/yellow/section/1/action/0: Clear one small surface or move one item to make space.
mini-reset/yellow/section/1/action/1: Tidy your focus area for 2 minutes—set a soft timer.
mini-reset/yellow/section/1/action/2: Switch to a different chair, surface, or part of the room.
mini-reset/yellow/section/1/action/3: Put on calming background music, a lo-fi playlist, or nature sounds.
mini-reset/yellow/section/1/action/4: Open a window or door for a breath of fresh air.
mini-reset/yellow/section/2/action/0: Write down one task you *do* want to do (not just what you should do).
mini-reset/yellow/section/2/action/1: Open the tab or app you need—don’t do anything else yet.
mini-reset/yellow/section/2/action/2: Text a friend or supportive person and let them know you’re regrouping.
mini-reset/yellow/section/2/action/3: Re-read your daily intention or revisit your Weekly Horizon.
mini-reset/yellow/section/2/action/4: Stand up, shake out your hands, and whisper: “Okay—next right thing.”
mini-reset/yellow/reentry: Feeling a little more grounded? Let’s head back in and choose something small to begin with.
quiz/o1/prompt: When planning a vacation, do you:
quiz/o1/option:A: Pick a new place you've never been
quiz/o1/option:B: Think about trying new activities in a place you know
quiz/o1/option:C: Prefer to go somewhere familiar and comfortable
quiz/o2/prompt: When given a school project with no specific guidelines, do you:
quiz/o2/option:A: Get excited about creating something unique
quiz/o2/option:B: Feel comfortable trying a new approach but still need some structure
quiz/o2/option:C: Prefer more detailed instructions to follow
quiz/o3/prompt: If your friends suggest trying a new activity you’ve never heard of, do you:
quiz/o3/option:A: Jump at the chance to try it
quiz/o3/option:B: Need some convincing but are open to it
quiz/o3/option:C: Prefer sticking to activities you already know
quiz/o4/prompt: When you see an unusual or unique idea, do you:
quiz/o4/option:A: Feel excited to learn more about it
quiz/o4/option:B: Consider it if others show interest
quiz/o4/option:C: Prefer to stick to familiar ideas
quiz/c1/prompt: When you have a set schedule, do you:
quiz/c1/option:A: Stick to it no matter what
quiz/c1/option:B: Follow it but allow for some flexibility
quiz/c1/option:C: Prefer to go with the flow and not stick to a strict schedule
quiz/c2/prompt: When you have a big assignment, do you:
quiz/c2/option:A: Plan it out carefully in advance
quiz/c2/option:B: Make a plan but adjust as needed
quiz/c2/option:C: Tackle it as it comes without a set plan
quiz/c3/prompt: When you have multiple tasks to complete, do you:
quiz/c3/option:A: Prioritize and tackle them one by one
quiz/c3/option:B: Make a list but stay flexible
quiz/c3/option:C: Handle them as they come without a specific order
quiz/c4/prompt: When working on a group project, do you:
quiz/c4/option:A: Prefer to plan everything in detail
quiz/c4/option:B: Plan but stay open to changes
quiz/c4/option:C: Go with the flow and adjust as needed
quiz/c5/prompt: When it comes to organizing your workspace, do you:
quiz/c5/option:B: Keep it organized but not rigidly
quiz/c5/option:C: Don't mind a bit of mess as long as you can find things
quiz/c6/prompt: When it comes to your room, do you:
quiz/c6/option:A: Need it to be tidy to focus
quiz/c6/option:B: Prefer it organized but can handle a bit of mess
quiz/c6/option:C: Feel comfortable even if it's a little messy
quiz/c7/prompt: Do you prepare for the next day by:
quiz/c7/option:B: Having a rough idea but leaving room for adjustments
quiz/c7/option:C: Going with the flow and seeing what the day brings
quiz/e1/prompt: When you attend social events, do you:
quiz/e1/option:A: Feel energized and love meeting new people
quiz/e1/option:B: Enjoy them but prefer smaller gatherings
quiz/e1/option:C: Find them draining and prefer quieter activities
quiz/e2/prompt: When working on a team project, do you:
quiz/e2/option:A: Take the lead and enjoy coordinating with others
quiz/e2/option:B: Prefer to collaborate but let others lead
quiz/e2/option:C: Prefer working alone or in small groups
quiz/e3/prompt: In a social setting, do you:
quiz/e3/option:A: Enjoy being the center of attention
quiz/e3/option:B: Like participating but not being the focus
quiz/e4/prompt: When meeting new people, do you:
quiz/e4/option:B: Feel comfortable but let them approach you
quiz/e4/option:C: Prefer to wait until you're introduced
quiz/a1/prompt: When working with others, do you:
quiz/a1/option:B: Usually cooperate but stand your ground when needed
quiz/a1/option:C: Prefer to stick to your own ideas
quiz/a2/prompt: When someone disagrees with you, do you:
quiz/a2/option:A: Listen and try to understand their perspective
quiz/a2/option:B: Consider their point but stick to yours
quiz/a2/option:C: Find it hard to agree and prefer your own view
quiz/a3/prompt: If a friend asks for help, do you:
quiz/a3/option:C: Prefer to focus on your own tasks
quiz/a4/prompt: When it comes to helping out at home or on a team, do you:
quiz/a4/option:A: Volunteer to help whenever you can
quiz/a4/option:C: Prefer not to get involved unless necessary
quiz/a5/prompt: When playing a team sport, do you:
quiz/a5/option:A: Always support and encourage your teammates
quiz/a5/option:B: Support them but focus on your own role
quiz/r1/prompt: When faced with a difficult situation, do you:
quiz/r1/option:A: Stay calm and find a solution
quiz/r1/option:B: Feel stressed but manage to cope
quiz/r2/prompt: When you experience a setback, do you:
quiz/r2/option:A: Bounce back quickly and keep going
quiz/r2/option:B: Feel upset but recover with time
quiz/r2/option:C: Struggle to move on and need encouragement
quiz/r3/prompt: When you're feeling anxious, do you:
quiz/r3/option:A: Find ways to calm yourself down
quiz/r3/option:B: Feel anxious but manage to get through it
quiz/r3/option:C: Need support from others to feel better
quiz/r4/prompt: When you face unexpected changes, do you:
quiz/r4/option:B: Feel uneasy but manage to adjust
quiz/r4/option:C: Struggle to cope and feel anxious
quiz/r5/prompt: When you're in a social situation and something unexpected happens, do you:
quiz/r5/option:B: Feel uneasy but manage to get through it
quiz/r5/option:C: Get anxious and prefer to leave
router/option:green: I’ve got energy and a plan in my head
router/option:yellow: I know what to do but I can’t start
safety/crisis/intro: Before we go any further — what you just shared sounds heavy, and it matters more than any plan we could make today.
safety/crisis/boundary: I’m not a counselor, but here’s someone who can help.
safety/crisis/resource/0: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — Call or text 988 — free, 24/7 (US)
safety/crisis/resource/1: Crisis Text Line — Text HOME to 741741 — free, 24/7 (US)
safety/crisis/trusted-adult: Is there an adult you trust — a parent, a teacher, a counselor — you could talk to today? You don’t have to carry this alone.
